mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware and integration with popular models. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This adds the specific vLLM release repository.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to function correctly within Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This installs the required CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs vLLM into the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM instance to verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This creates a simple vLLM instance using the GPT-2 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'

```

> Should return 'True' if the vLLM instance is ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step will remove vLLM and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the vLLM instance and any data associated with it.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls vLLM from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain example-vllm --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl uncordon example-vllm

```

> This command drains the vLLM instance.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> This command uncordons the vLLM instance to allow scheduling.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' and ensure the vLLM repository is added correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CustomResourceDefinition already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for vLLM is already installed.

**Fix:**
Check existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd' and remove if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: vLLM instance is not ready
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM instance may be in a crash loop or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for errors and adjust configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server may be unreachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is set up correctly and the cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required from earlier versions.

resolution.summary: You should see 'True' when running 'kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'', indicating the vLLM instance is ready.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
